rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MENDALA VS. IRENE CHEUNG, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Document Production Req. To Deft Tiempo Interiors, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 21, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF ROBERT MENDALA'S MOTION To Compel Further Responses To PLAINTIFF'S Document Production ReqUEST To DefENDANt Tiempo Interiors, Set Two. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED in part. Tax returns and related documents kept by Defendant's accountant are deemed to be within its custody and control. The documents are relevant to the Tameny claims. Defendant shall produce its tax returns for 2004, 2005 and 2006. Defendant has failed to distinguish Schnabel v. Sup. Ct. (1993) 5 Cal 4th 704. The records and data upon which tax returns are based are subject to discovery. See Weil & Brown et al. CAL PRAC. GUIDE:CIV PRO. BEFORE TRIAL (TRG 2011) § 8:122. Plaintiff's request for all documents "concerning" tax returns is overbroad. Defendant must produce: (1) all bank statements of Tiempo Interiors, Inc. sufficient to show all deposits made in 2004, 2005 and 2006; (2) any other documents recording cash receipts of Tiempo Interiors, Inc. not deposited into a bank account in 2004, 2005, and 2006. If Plaintiff believes these documents are insufficient to attempt to prove his claims, counsel may appear at the hearing to seek to modify this order. All documents must be produced by March 3, 2013. Sanctions are awarded to Plaintiff in the amount of $840. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JAVIER AVELLANEDA VS. MG-301 MISSION LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 21, 2013, Line 11, PLAINTIFF JAVIER AVELLANEDA'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint Denied. Plaintiff seeks to add Mr. Martinez as a new plaintiff with distinct facts. Prejudice will result to Defendant by the addition of a new plaintiff less than three weeks before trial and after the close of discovery. Further, Plaintiff concedes that Mr. Martinez may file a separate action for his claims. The motion is also denied to the extent that Plaintiff, in his reply brief, seeks to add two new causes of action. This ground for amendment was not raised in the moving papers, which, to the contrary, explicitly stated that Plaintiff was not adding any new causes of action in the First Amended Complaint. (MPA at 3, lines 8-9.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL A. JACKSON et al VS. MARY A. HUGHES, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Prodcution Of Documents Propounded On Pltf And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 21, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 3, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For ProduCtion Of Documents Propounded On PlAINTIFF And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part and denied in part. The essence of Plaintiffs' specific objections to production of documents is that the servers have been tampered with. While that may be true, that does not render responsive documents non-discoverable. Whether or not those documents are admissible at trial is an entirely different question, which is not before this Court. If there are privileged documents, they must be specifically identified in a privilege log. The motion is denied with respect to Document Request 14, which seeks the production of the settlement agreement in a related case. It does not appear that this confidential document is likely to be relevant in supporting either party's case. The Request for Judicial Notice is granted. The objections to evidence are denied as moot. The Court did not consider any objectionable evidence in arriving at this order. Plaintiff is ordered to serve amended responses to Requests 5-8, 10-13, 15-27, 28-36 and 38 and to produce responsive documents within ten days. Sanctions against Gabriel Jackson and her attorneys are ordered in the amount of $5,000. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al VS. RAUL AMILCAR VASQUEZ, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 21, 2013, Line 19, Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction Granted. Plaintiffs have shown they are likely to succeed on the merits. Defendants do not dispute the code violations. The Court finds sufficient evidence that the premises are being used to operate an auto repair business, and, even if this activity has recently stopped, there is no harm to Defendants in enjoining them from further illegal operation of this business on the premises. Defendants have not shown grave or irreparable harm from the issuance of the preliminary injunction. The Court will sign the proposed preliminary injunction submitted by Plaintiffs, unless the parties have agreed to a modified order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
